steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the relationship between the two texts. Text 1 ends negatively: the whoop 'could be more irritating than enjoyable.' Text 2 concedes it was overused but argues it 'could also be genuinely fun or even moving' and 'was never used well' is too strong. The relationship is one of contrasting judgments.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Text 1 provides an explanation of events that inspired a song, while Text 2 offers a different explanation of those events.': Neither text explains what inspired 'And We Danced'; both evaluate the whoop pattern itself. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Text 1 introduces a negative view of a musical pattern, while Text 2 presents a more positive view of that pattern.': Text 1 calls the whoop irritating; Text 2 defends it as sometimes fun or moving. This is exactly the contrast. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Text 1 discusses the advantages of a technique for recording music, while Text 2 discusses the problems with that technique.': Text 1 is negative, not positive; and neither text is about recording techniques. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Text 1 focuses on critics who praised a particular style of music, while Text 2 focuses on critics who disapproved of that style.': Neither text discusses critics; both offer the authors' own assessments, and Text 1's view is negative, not praising. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
